audit_metadata.scope: ClimbMatch database - Washington State climbing routes

executive_summary.overall_assessment: Database has solid GPS foundation and reasonable hazard coverage, but requires urgent permit data verification for 2026 currency and significant hazard enhancement for specificity.

key_findings: 52% of hazards well-documented with location, season, condition, and action
48% of hazards need enhancement - missing critical elements
Generic descriptions without specific location markers are most common gap
Seasonal windows missing in 40%+ of entries
Actionable guidance missing in 48%+ of entries
priority_routes_for_enhancement: Mount Stuart (The Gendarme) - rockfall, exposure, weather
Mount Rainier glacier routes (8 routes) - serac/crevasse specificity
Early Winters Spires (6 routes) - weather, route-finding
Liberty Bell Group (5 routes) - exposure, loose rock

phase_2_permits.critical_finding: ALL 34 AUDITED ENTRIES FLAGGED FOR 2026 VERIFICATION

critical_closures: Mowich Lake area - SR 165 bridge closed, no alternate route - COMPLETELY INACCESSIBLE
Grove of the Patriarchs Trail - closed through 2027+
Carbon River area - 5-mile walk-in required (drive-up access not available)
Tahoma Creek Trail - debris flow hazard, closed
Timed entry pilot program - CANCELLED for 2026
Cascade Pass/Sahale Glacier - no advance reservations before July 1, 2026

usfs_permits.status: Requires direct verification via USFS websites
pending_verification: Alpine Lakes Wilderness - still USFS self-issue, trailhead locations need update
Glacier Peak Wilderness - 2026 policy confirmation needed
Pasayten Wilderness - fee and quota status

database_discrepancies_critical.issue: Mowich Lake ranger station listed as operational
database_discrepancies_critical.reason: SR 165 bridge closed indefinitely - no alternate route - COMPLETELY INACCESSIBLE
database_discrepancies_critical.action: Remove ranger station reference or mark inaccessible

database_discrepancies_critical.reason: Database may still reference old flat $20 fee; correct 2026 fee is $10/person/night
database_discrepancies_critical.routes_affected: All Mount Rainier wilderness camping routes

database_discrepancies_critical.issue: Carbon River access assumes drive-up parking
database_discrepancies_critical.reason: Carbon River Road closed since 2006; 5-mile walk-in required to Ipsut Creek Campground

validation_results.geographic_bounds_check: All coordinates within WA State (45.5-49.1°N, -124.8 to -116.9°W)
validation_results.elevation_consistency: All high-point elevations reasonable relative to peak elevation
validation_results.placeholder_detection: No (0,0) or obviously fake coordinates detected
validation_results.decimal_precision: Appropriate for route-level accuracy (4-5 decimal places = 10-1m precision)

phase_3_gps.assessment: GPS coordinates show professional data quality - likely OpenBeta import or expert data entry

peak_incident_activity: Mount Stuart - rockfall incidents 2024-2025, loose rock conditions
Liberty Bell Group - route-finding incidents during fog 2024-2025
Early Winters Spires - weather incidents above treeline 2024
Alpine Lakes peaks - navigation incidents, commitment underestimation
hazard_documentation_gaps: Glacier/serac hazards lack specific seasonal opening dates (typically July 1)
Rockfall hazards don't specify which pitches/time windows show highest risk
Weather hazards lack altitude thresholds and specific afternoon windows
Route-finding hazards missing landmark descriptions and fog-weather guidance
Commitment/endurance hazards underestimate time requirements on long approaches

critical_immediate.action: Remove or mark 'Mowich Lake ranger station' as inaccessible
critical_immediate.impact: Prevents user misguidance to inaccessible location

critical_immediate.action: Update Mount Rainier wilderness permit fees to $10/person/night
Verify and update all USFS permit information
critical_immediate.reason: 100% of sampled entries flagged for 2026 verification

high_priority.action: Add clear warning about Carbon River 5-mile walk-in
Enhance 10 highest-priority hazard routes with structured details
high_priority.reason: Mount Stuart, Rainier, Early Winters routes see most incidents

high_priority.action: Add 2026 North Cascades permit lottery dates
high_priority.reason: Climbers need March 2-13 early-access lottery dates for planning
Enables consistent maintenance and user clarity
high_priority.format: System | Fee | Location | Phone | Website | Valid_Dates

medium_priority.reason: Ensure comprehensive hazard documentation across all routes
Enables better hazard tracking, filtering, and verification
medium_priority.schema: Separate hazard_details array with type, location, season, severity, consequences, mitigation

permit_information.status: All 34 audited entries flagged; 100% need 2026 verification

incident_alignment.status: Several hazards under-documented relative to incident frequency

overall_quality.summary: Solid GPS foundation; good hazard existence; poor permit currency; medium hazard specificity. Needs urgent permit updates and targeted hazard enhancements.

research_agents_final_status.permit_research: COMPLETE - NPS Mount Rainier & North Cascades verified; USFS permits identified for direct verification
research_agents_final_status.incident_research: COMPLETE - 2024-2026 NPS incidents documented, hazard misalignments identified
